input.description: Find the target test and the sibling migrated test

data.output:
700			f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
701			f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
702			root := f.NewRootCommand()
703			out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "shared-bead", "--json",
704				"--from", baseSHA,
705				"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
706			require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
707			return parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
708		}
709	
710		res1 := makeRun()
711		res2 := makeRun()
712	
713		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res1.Outcome)
714		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res2.Outcome)
715		assert.NotEqual(t, res1.PreserveRef, res2.PreserveRef,
716			"concurrent runs must produce distinct preserve refs")
717		// Real Land() records the ref as refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<attempt>-<currentTip[:12]>.
718		// The first run's currentTip is siblingSHA; the second run's tip differs
719		// because execute-bead checkpoints tracker/worktree state between runs.
720		require.Regexp(t,
721			fmt.Sprintf(`^refs/ddx/iterations/shared-bead/.+-%s$`, regexp.QuoteMeta(siblingSHA[:12])),
722			res1.PreserveRef)
723		require.Regexp(t, `^refs/ddx/iterations/shared-bead/.+-[0-9a-f]{12}$`, res2.PreserveRef)
724	
725		// Both hidden refs must exist in the real repo and resolve to their
726		// respective worker commits.
727		assert.Equal(t, res1.ResultRev, runGit("rev-parse", res1.PreserveRef))
728		assert.Equal(t, res2.ResultRev, runGit("rev-parse", res2.PreserveRef))
729	}
730	
731	// TestExecuteBeadNoChanges verifies that when the agent makes no commits the
732	// outcome is "no-changes". Exercises real git + the script harness: the
733	// directive file tells the harness to no-op, so execute-bead observes an
734	// unchanged worktree HEAD and classifies the run accordingly.
735	func TestExecuteBeadNoChanges(t *testing.T) {
736		workDir := t.TempDir()
737	
738		scrubEnv := func() []string {
739			parent := os.Environ()
740			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
741			for _, kv := range parent {
742				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
743					continue
744				}
745				env = append(env, kv)
746			}
747			return env
748		}
749		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
750			t.Helper()
751			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
752			cmd.Dir = workDir
753			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
754			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
755			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
756			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
757		}
758	
759		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
760		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
761		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
762		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
763		runGit("add", ".")
764		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
765	
766		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
767			ID:        "my-bead",
768			Title:     "Test execute-bead no-changes outcome",
769			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
770			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
771		})
772		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
773		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
774		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
775	
776		// Script harness does nothing — no file creation, no commit.
777		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
778		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte("no-op\n"), 0o644))
779	
780		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
781		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
782		root := f.NewRootCommand()
783		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
784			"--from", baseSHA,
785			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
786		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
787		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
788	
789		assert.Equal(t, "no-changes", res.Outcome)
790		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, res.Status)
791		assert.Equal(t, baseSHA, res.BaseRev)
792		assert.Empty(t, res.PreserveRef)
793	}
794	
795	// TestExecuteBeadDirtyWorktreeWithoutCommits verifies that tracked file edits
796	// left uncommitted by the agent are synthesized into a commit and treated as
797	// real output rather than being discarded as "no-changes".
798	func TestExecuteBeadDirtyWorktreeWithoutCommits(t *testing.T) {
799		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
800			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
801			wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111", // agent made no commits
802			wtDirty:     true,       // but left tracked file edits
803			synthRev:    "cccc3333", // SynthesizeCommit produces this rev
804		}
805		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
806		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
807	
808		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
809	
810		assert.NotEqual(t, "no-changes", res.Outcome, "dirty worktree should not be classified as no-changes")
811		assert.Equal(t, "cccc3333", res.ResultRev)
812		assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.BaseRev)
813		assert.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
814		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
815		assert.Equal(t, 1, git.mergeCalls)
816		assert.Equal(t, "cccc3333", git.mergeRev)
817	}
818	
819	func TestExecuteBeadMergePreservesContext(t *testing.T) {
820		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
821			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
822			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
823			mergeErr:    nil,
824		}
825		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
826		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
827	
828		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
829	
830		assert.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
831		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
832		assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.BaseRev)
833		assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", res.ResultRev)
834		assert.Equal(t, 1, git.mergeCalls)
835		assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", git.mergeRev)
836	}
837	
838	func TestExecuteBeadSynthesizesPromptAndArtifacts(t *testing.T) {
839		workDir := t.TempDir()
840		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
841			ID:          "my-bead",
842			Title:       "Improve execute-bead prompt synthesis",
843			Status:      bead.StatusOpen,
844			Priority:    0,
845			IssueType:   bead.DefaultType,
846			Description: "Replace the bare fallback prompt with deterministic bead context.",
847			Acceptance:  "Prompt contains bead context and governing references.",
848			Labels:      []string{"area:agent", "phase:build"},
849			Extra:       map[string]any{"spec-id": "FEAT-006"},
850		})
851		specPath := filepath.Join(workDir, "docs", "feature.md")
852		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(specPath), 0o755))
853		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(specPath, []byte(`---
854	ddx:
855	  id: FEAT-006
856	---
857	# Agent Service
858	`), 0o644))
859	
860		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
861			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
862			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
863		}
864		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}
865		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
866		seedDefaultExecuteBeads(t, workDir)
867		f.AgentRunnerOverride = runner
868		f.executeBeadGitOverride = git
869		f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride = git
870		f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride = fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit(git)
871	
872		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
873	
874		require.NotEmpty(t, runner.last.PromptFile)
875		require.FileExists(t, runner.last.PromptFile)
876		promptRaw, err := os.ReadFile(runner.last.PromptFile)
877		require.NoError(t, err)
878		promptText := string(promptRaw)
879		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Improve execute-bead prompt synthesis")
880		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Replace the bare fallback prompt")
881		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Prompt contains bead context and governing references.")
882		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "docs/feature.md")
883		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "Work on bead my-bead.")
884	
885		require.NotEmpty(t, res.ExecutionDir)
886		require.NotEmpty(t, res.PromptFile)
887		require.NotEmpty(t, res.ManifestFile)
888		require.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultFile)
889		assert.True(t, strings.HasSuffix(res.PromptFile, "prompt.md"))
890		assert.True(t, strings.HasSuffix(res.ManifestFile, "manifest.json"))
891		assert.True(t, strings.HasSuffix(res.ResultFile, "result.json"))
892	}
893	
894	func TestExecuteBeadResolvesPathStyleSpecID(t *testing.T) {
895		workDir := t.TempDir()
896		specPath := filepath.Join(workDir, "workflows", "README.md")
897		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(specPath), 0o755))
898		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(specPath, []byte("# Workflow\n"), 0o644))
899		refs := agent.ResolveGoverningRefs(workDir, &bead.Bead{

23	// fakeExecuteBeadGit is a mock implementation of executeBeadGitOps for tests.
24	type fakeExecuteBeadGit struct {
25		mu sync.Mutex
26	
27		// mainHeadRev is returned by HeadRev/ResolveRev for the main working dir.
28		mainHeadRev string
29		// headRevSeq, when set, is returned in order for successive main-dir HeadRev calls.
30		headRevSeq []string
31		headRevIdx int
32		// wtHeadRev is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths (after agent run).
33		wtHeadRev string
34		// wtDirty is returned by IsDirty for worktree paths.
35		wtDirty bool
36		// synthRev, if set, is applied as wtHeadRev when SynthesizeCommit is called.
37		synthRev string
38		// wtHeadRevErr, if set, is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths.
39		wtHeadRevErr error
40		dirty        bool
41		mergeErr     error
42		updateRefErr error
43	
44		addedWTs   []string
45		addedWTRev string
46		removedWTs []string
47		refs       map[string]string // ref -> sha recorded by UpdateRef
48		worktrees  []string          // paths returned by WorktreeList
49	
50		mergeCalls int
51		mergeRev   string
52	}
53	
54	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
55		f.mu.Lock()
56		defer f.mu.Unlock()
57		if strings.Contains(dir, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix) {
58			if f.wtHeadRevErr != nil {
59				return "", f.wtHeadRevErr
60			}
